channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.rockpapershotgun.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.rockpapershotgun.com

item.title: Microsoft Israel boss leaves after investigation into whether IDF contracts broke Microsoft's code of ethics
item.content:encoded:
The general manager of Microsoft Israel, Alon Haimovich, has left his role following an investigation into the regional branch's contracts with the Israeli Defense Forces' intelligence units.

According to a report by Globes, Microsoft's global management launched the investigation because they were concerned that the company's code of ethics had been breached by the branch's work with the IDF. While Microsoft have not revealed the results of the investigation they announced in August, Globes reports that it is behind Haimovich's exit from the company, along with several other senior members of Microsoft Israel's governance department.

In January last year, The Guardian and +972 Magazine reported that Unit 8200, a surveillance division within the IDF, had developed technology used to track Palestinians in the West Bank and Gaza which recorded their positions and phone calls. This technology was allegedly used to create a database of potential targets for lethal strikes. While the Israeli government have a contract with Amazon and Google to build datacentre infrastructure within the country, Microsoft are not a partner in the Nimbus project, which means that data collected by the IDF intelligence units would be stored on Azure servers located in Europe, opening Microsoft up to greater regulatory scrutiny.

Nine months after The Guardian and +972 Magazine's reporting, significant protests within Microsoft and a growing boycott against their products, led Microsoft to cancel their contract with Unit 8200. However, they did not unilaterally cancel their work with the IDF. According to Globes, the Microsoft investigation into the Israel branch's contracts revealed that Unit 8200 were not the only unit that violated Microsoft's terms of use. In their words, it was just the "tip of the iceberg".

Microsoft have not announced the termination of any other contracts with the IDF and, for now, the Israel branch of their offices is being managed by Microsoft France. Though, Globes reports that IDF units have already transferred a significant part of their cloud infrastructure to the datacentres constructed by Amazon and Google within Israel's borders, where they face less international oversight.

The existence of this investigation into Microsoft Israel's dealings with the IDF is in part a result of the protests and boycotts that have been mounted against the corporation since last year. However, as Edwin has reported extensively, Microsoft workers taking part in the boycott and campaign against the corporation's work with the IDF have faced what they've described as "a culture of intimidation, retaliation, and oppression". Damage which won't be undone because of Haimovich's exit.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/microsoft-israel-boss-leaves-after-investigation-into-whether-idf-contracts-broke-microsofts-code-of-ethics
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Wordle's getting a TV adaptation made in partnership with Jimmy Fallon’s Electric Hot Dog, please pray for our guides writers
item.content:encoded:
Seriously, keep them in your thoughts

Wordle. A lot of people play it. In turn, a lot of guides writers at sites across the internet have to play it as part of their jobs, so that they can provide that big bunch of players with answers and helpful hints. It's relentless work that's invaluable in helping keep the likes of us full-time newsers and critics in jobs and one that might now end up leading these talented folks to have to bravely do something you wouldn't wish on your worst enemy - watching a TV show about regular people solving word puzzles that's being made in partnership with Jimmy Fallon’s production company.

I can hear the screams from outside of the RPS editorial cupboard already, as they read this report from Wordle owners the New York Times. NBC have greenlit a primetime game show based on Wordle, it reads, adding that the show will "feature teams of players competing to solve five-letter word puzzles in a high-energy, collaborative environment". The screams are only getting louder.

Universal Television Alternative Studio is producing it, in partnership with Jimmy Fallon’s Electric Hot Dog and The New York Times. The screams are nearly deafening now. Savannah Guthrie, co-anchor of NBC's morning show Today - in which a number of American anchors with very white teeth and very formal attire discuss things happening, er, today - is set to host. The screams have stayed at a similar decibel level there, I assume because they're not familiar with Guthrie's work and want to give her a chance in this new gig.

"As we met with different production companies, it became clear that Jimmy Fallon and Jim Juvonen at Electric Hot Dog really understood what makes Wordle special," Caitlin Roper, the NYT's executive editorial director of film and TV, said. "They bring strong game development experience both on 'The Tonight Show,' where they’ve explored all kinds of gameplay, and in their other shows like 'That’s My Jam' and 'Password,' but also a real appreciation for the original game of Wordle, which was super important to us. Jimmy plays Wordle every day with his daughters. And he’s such an ambassador of gameplay, inviting all kinds of people in to play alongside him. Savannah absolutely loves Wordle and she is also really good at it."

Ah no, the screams have gone up in volume again. I'm going to link our past Wordle answers page and hope that helps them calm down a bit. I'm too scared to poke my head out of the cupboard and check, lest Jeremy, Callum, and Ollie rip me out and begin to tear my paper-thin body to pieces in blind panic and rage.

Hopefully the screaming stops soon. I fear the headlines, indie games, and hardware I've dished out to my cupboard mates Julian, Edwin, and James - in order to keep them from being freaked out by the cacophony - last long enough. While I wait with bated breath, you can apply to be a contestant on the Wordle show here, assuming you're eligible.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/wordles-getting-a-tv-adaptation-made-in-partnership-with-jimmy-fallons-electric-hot-dog-please-pray-for-our-guides-writers
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
